examples: Their allele frequencies at numerous genetic loci gradually become more and more different as new alleles independently arise by mutation in each population.
Instead, each allele of the gene sets out a series of probabilities.
The risk gene divides the world into two camps -those who have the FTO allele and those who don't.
People with AD are more likely to have an APOE e4 allele than people who do not develop AD.
However, many people with AD do not have an APOE e4 allele.
